name: STL (Seasonal-Trend decomposition using LOESS)
applicable_scenarios: separate a univariate series into seasonal, trend, and residual components
detect anomalies by inspecting the residual after removing seasonality
python_template:
import numpy as np
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import STL

def stl_decompose(y, period=12, seasonal=7, robust=True):
    stl = STL(y, period=period, seasonal=seasonal, robust=robust)
    result = stl.fit()
    return result  # .trend, .seasonal, .resid
typical_cases: decomposing monthly CO2 series into annual cycle + long-term trend
pre-processing step for residual-based anomaly detection
visualizing growth trend under strong seasonality
common_pitfalls: requires the seasonal period to be known a priori
non-robust STL is sensitive to outliers — use robust=True when in doubt
short series (< 2 periods) cannot be decomposed reliably
residuals are not guaranteed to be white noise — check ACF
